frameworks.name: fMRI-based alertness marker
frameworks.one_line: Continuous time-resolved alertness extracted from fMRI signal alone

frameworks.name: arousal state modulation
frameworks.one_line: Ongoing alertness fluctuations shape neural and behavioral responses

frameworks.name: trial-to-trial behavioral variability
frameworks.one_line: Explains response variance via prestimulus alertness state

core_question: Can a continuous alertness marker be extracted from fMRI alone and predict trial-by-trial behavioral variability?
key_assumptions: Alertness leaves a recoverable fMRI signature without EEG or pupillometry
A short pre-stimulus interval suffices to index ongoing arousal
A small voxel subset carries the relevant alertness information
manipulations: Extracted pre-stimulus alertness marker from fMRI
Compared task-evoked responses with and without alertness covariate
Tested prediction across full-brain vs sparse voxel subsets
key_findings: Pre-stimulus fMRI alertness marker captures trial-to-trial behavioral responses
EEG and behavioral prediction achievable from a small fraction of fMRI voxels
Accounting for alertness increases statistical detection of task-activated areas
interpretations: fMRI alone can recover ongoing arousal information previously requiring EEG or pupillometry
Existing fMRI datasets can be enriched with alertness covariates to study neural variability
connecting_signals: neural variability in health and disease
